name: Friday Freight Train
description:
Join Team Endurance Nation for a great weekend kick off ride! This session focuses on steady state riding and is a great way to build up some mileage. All riders are welcome to join this event and be part of our group riding community within Zwift. Feel free to keep riding after the finish to rack up more miles with the group!

Warm Up: Very short. Just enough to build the group.
Main Ride: Steady riding at 2.5 w/kg. Overall average including climbs and finish should be 2.7 w/kg.

TeamEN members chat on an app called Discord. Discord is not required, but the ride experience is enhanced by using it. Click on this link for more information about Discord and related setup: https://www.endurancenation.us/teamen-on-zwift#discord

For more specifics about this ride and various other Endurance Nation rides, go to: https://www.endurancenation.us/teamen-on-zwift

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Group Run 10k
description:
Join us for a 10k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends. #pbt

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

name: SROZ's No Stress Express
description:
Leave the ego at the starting pen!								
Whether you’re a total beginner finding your legs, a veteran rider flushing out the lactic acid on a recovery day, or just someone who wants to enjoy the scenery without staring at their power meter—this is your ride.								
Why join the Express?								
We use Zwift’s "Keep Together" (banded) feature. As long as you keep pedaling, you’ll stay with the group regardless of your power output. 								
Zero Drop Policy: No one gets left behind. Period.								
								
This is a chat-heavy ride. Connect with fellow Zwifters, share tips, or just listen to the hum of the virtual tarmac.								
								
All Abilities Welcome: From 0.5 w/kg to 5.0 w/kg, everyone is equal on the Express!

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: DBR Friday Race
description:
Welcome to DBR Friday Race.
All will start with 1 minute between RS categories. Expect each race to be highly competitive and have fun.
Categories
Each race will be using Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) for categorization.
race up a category, if your RS is at the top end of a category. Or if you simply desire an added challenge.
- A: 1000 =< 690
- B:690<520
- C:520<350
- D:350<180
- E:180<=0
Your Zwift Racing Score is determined by how much success you have in races.
better against people with higher ZRS, then your ZRS may increase. If you place worse than people with lower ZRS, then your score might decrease. Read more about ZRS here - https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh <https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh>
If your ZRS increases past a category boundary during the series, your points earned will not transfer to the new category with you.
RULES:
Smart trainer or power meter is required. zPower will be removed.
Heart rate monitors are compulsory for all categories.
No TT bikes unless it's a TT event.
You need to have a registered account with Zwiftpower.com and accept their terms and conditions to be in the results of the race.
Misc.
Race organizers may request riders in all categories to provide a dual recording or a matching RL effort (Strava) in order to verify a Zwift race effort. Failure to comply may result in disqualification from the event. Failure to provide a dual recording or RL effort on three requests may result in disqualification from future events, until a dual recording with a matching effort is provided.
Race organizers may exclude a rider from the results, due to poor sportsmanship. Examples may be bad language, sandbagging or other behavior of a disruptive manner.

#danishbikeriders

name: Chasing SYNers Ride
description:
Your weekend starts here with the SYN—hold the wheel for 60 minutes, then race to the line. Every Friday the SYN Global Cycling Club brings you the Chasing SYNers Ride—a weekly community spin with a spicy finish. 

60MIN GROUP RIDE + RACE TO THE FINISH
The first hour is a solid C-paced group ride, guided and controlled by our SYN ride leaders to keep the pack smooth and tight. After roughly 60 minutes, the doors open. The final block is a free race to the line—sometimes a climb, sometimes a crit-style sprint, always a chance to empty the tank. It’s the perfect way to kick the weekend wide open with a serious push.

UNLOCK THE SYNDICATE CLUB KIT
Finish our Chasing SYNers Ride on Zwift and unlock the Inverted SYN Kit (black jersey, white bib) in your garage, so you can wear the colors of our cycling club in any other ride on Zwift.

DISCOVER MORE IN THE SYN ZWIFT CLUB
Enjoyed this ride? Feel free to join our Zwift Club (SYN Cycling Club), and discover all our events:
https://bit.ly/SYNZwiftClub

name: Team DRAFT Friday Race
description:
WHAT IS CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT?

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for. Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You can not race in the D category.

Welcome to the Friday Race brought to you by Team DRAFT!

We encourage all participants to head over to zwiftpower.com and register. Riders who have not completed the Zwiftpower registration and agreed to the sharing conditions will not be included in the official results.

To make this race as fair as possible, we require that all riders wear a heart rate monitor, use a power meter and/or smart trainer, and choose the appropriate race category for your ability. Riding in an inappropriate category will lead to your disqualification.

RULES:
- No zPower riders.
- Heart rate monitors mandatory for all categories
- 5min and 20min CEVAZ limits apply. Riding in the wrong category will lead to disqualification.

RACE CATEGORIES:
A: 4.0 W/kg to 5.0 W/kg
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg
D: Under 2.49 W/kg

name: Fast Friday Workout
description:
Congratulations, you crushed another week of workouts! If you’re looking to cap off a great week with another can't-miss training session, check out Fast Friday on Zwift. 

A new workout series to put in your weekly run rotation, Fast Friday is a simple and streamlined way to get your heart rate up. We’re focusing on short reps here, clocking in fast intervals that help runners increase their top speed. Short. Sharp. Fast. High-intensity intervals are proven to take your performance to the next level. So lace up and get moving for just 20–30 minutes every Friday before checking out of the office and into the weekend. 

#fastfriday

description:
The working day is over for this week and it's time to go on the weekend.
But before you sit down on the couch in front of the TV, we end with a real race together with Team SZR.

This race is a chase race, where you chase the groups in front of you D:s start first and then the rest of the categories.
The goal here is to work together in the group and hopefully all groups meetup at the end on the finishline

RACE RULES
~ No TT bikes
~ Heart rate monitors are compulsory for all categories.
~ power ups are disabled
~ zPower riders will be removed from the results
~ All riders must have a Zwiftpower account to be in the results
Results will be presented at Zwiftpower.com

description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: GXY DRAFT MONKEY [2.3-2.7WKG] CAT C
description:
Welcome to GXY Draft Monkey!

We will aim to keep this ride as tight as possible, creating a blob of riders – the tighter we are, the faster we ride!!

Please respect the leader’s pace and bring your best A game banter – we start and end the week with a blast on Draft Monkey ☺

GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.

If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:

https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

Email:

name: Vikings and Valhalla Friday Night Lights
description:
Welcome one and all to the Vikings and Valhalla Friday Night Lights group ride. It’s Friday evening for Europe and we want to kick the weekend off with a group ride to remember. Are you looking for a chilled atmosphere and a group that loves to work together, look no further as you have now found the perfect way to get your weekend groove on!

For more information about our group rides please check out our Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/503388660165279

eventSubgroups.name: Vikings and Valhalla Friday Night Lights (D)

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (FemmeCycle Collab)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by FemmeCycle Collab (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/260d0d7e-4b91-4162-b025-fd202b8a6e74).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #femmecycle

eventSeries.name: ZRL 25/26 R4
eventSeries.description: Zwift Racing League Season 20 (2025/26 - Round 4). Includes all races and official recon rides

name: Cycle Nation Friday Corkscrew Chase
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Friday Corkscrew Chase. Join us for an easy evening social ride or an exciting pursuit, depending on which yellow beacon you chose to ride with.
The first yellow beacon will lead the corks out at the start of the ride, and will ride at 1.8w/kg for the duration. The second yellow beacon will start 4 minutes after the start time, leading the screws at 2.8w/kg until they catch the first group. Then, with the refreshments uncorked the chase group party at a social pace to the finish line.
We encourage you to work with your chosen ride leader (yellow beacon) . We're looking forward to hosting you for the ride.

Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Twitter: https://bit.ly/3n4uhft
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: Bag that Badge Series
description:
Open to all runners, Bag that Badge series is hosted by Stephen Cousins and is a fun way to earn your Zwift route badges.
Stephen Cousins is an active member of the Zwift Runners Facebook group and founder of Film My Run (FMR).

Join the FMR Club here https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join

Watch the Live Stream of this event on the Zwift Run Channel at http://youtube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

FMR Events are sponsored by Noble Pro Treadmills. Get your 4% discount on any treadmill from the Noble Pro range here http://zwift.run/noble-pro

name: ZTPL Cycling Club 100km ride with Wintertal
description:
☀️Weszliśmy w sezon wiosenno-letni☀️
Część naszych eventów zostaje w kalendarzu, ale może zdarzyć się tak że nie będzie na nich naszych liderów jazd.
Jeśli tak się zdarzy, gorąca prośba do Was o jazdę wg. założeń danego eventu.
Nasze eventy i liderzy ZTPL Cycling Club wrócą ponownie na jesień 2026.
Póki co RIDE ON i korzystajcie z pogody, a jeśli nie możecie to bawcie się dobrze podczas naszych eventów.

Zapraszamy na wspólny event z ZTPL Cycling Club oraz naszego partnera WINTERTAL producenta odzieży casualowej dla rowerzystów.
◾️Nasze casualowe koszulki znajdziecie u Wintertala: https://wintertal.com/pl/133-ztplcc

Pamiętajcie o wyborze właściwej grupy.
Nasza piątkowa “100tka” pomoże Wam nauczyć się jeździć długo w określonych wartościach w/kg.
Tempo będzie w 99% procentach równe, ale to ostatecznie od liderów zależy czy pojawią się jakieś akcenty np na podjazdach. Podane założenia w/kg dotyczą średniej wartości z całej jazdy.

Z założenia nasze jazdy prowadzą liderzy, którzy pomagają trzymać peleton w ryzach. Jeśli podczas eventu zabraknie lidera, starajcie się trzymać tempo na które się umówiliśmy w momencie gdy dołączyliście do wybranej grupy. To od Was zależy jak będziecie się bawić 😊

Po ukończonym evencie, jeśli jechaliście z nami pierwszy raz, w Waszym wirtualnym garażu będzie czekała nasza teamowa koszulka!

◾️Grupa D jest grupą początkującą i będzie pokonywać 50 kilometrów. Tempo jest spokojne, uczymy się jazdy w grupie, jest czas żeby porozmawiać.
◾️ Grupa C przeznaczona jest dla średnio zaawansowanych. Tempo będzie oscylować w okolicach 2.6-3.2 w/kg z przodu peletonu (możliwe akcenty).
Ważne: Jeśli wasze FTP jest w granicach 2.8w/kg to nie będzie grupa dla Was
◾️ Grupa B przeznaczona jest dla bardziej zaawansowanych. Tempo będzie oscylować w okolicach 3-3.5w/kg dodatkowo mogą wystąpić akcenty.
Ważne: Jeśli wasze FTP jest w okolicach 3.2w/kg to może nie być grupa dla Was
◾️ Jeśli chcecie przejechać jazdę w tempie Coffee Ride wybierzcie o jedną grupę niżej niż sugeruje wam wasze FTP lub zwiftpower :)

◾️ Wartości podane w opisach dotyczą średniego tempa z CAŁEJ jazdy (nie uwzględniają interwałów, które mogą wystąpić podczas np. podjazdów)
◾️ PAMIĘTAJCIE że podczas jazd grupowych NIE UŻYWAMY ROWERÓW CZASOWYCH!
◾️ Starajcie się pilnować peletonu i nie zgubić koła, samemu bardzo trudno jest odrobić stratę.
◾️ Proszę słuchajcie poleceń lidera, które podczas jazdy wyświetlane są na środku ekranu.
◾️ Lider, który prowadzi grupę podświetlony jest na żółto.
◾️ Starajcie się jechać blisko lidera, starając się go nie wyprzedać i nie wyjeżdżać przed bramę, która informuje Was że jedziecie za szybko.
Proszę stosujcie się do poleceń lidera!
Szczególnie jeśli lider zauważy że grupa się porwała.

◾️Jeśli chcecie sprawić sobie już teraz naszą nową teamową koszulkę ZTPL Cycling Club zapraszamy bezpośrednio na stronę https://inoni.cc/?s=ztpl&post_type=product
◾️Cała gama naszych koszulek oraz informacje o kolejnych Pre-Orderach znajdziecie na https://ztpl.cc/stroje-ztpl-cc/

◾️Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/ZwiftPolska
◾️Grupa na facebooku: Polska Społeczność Zwift https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwift.polska
◾️Instagram: #ZTPLCyclingClub https://www.instagram.com/ztpl.cc/
◾️Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/182377
◾️Nasz klub w Companionie: https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/ztplcc
◾️Nasze wszystkie klubowe jazdy: https://www.zwift.com/eu/events/tag/ztplcc
◾️Nasza strona internetowa: https://ztpl.cc/

#RideOn #ZTPLCC

[ENG]
☀️We’re now in the spring and summer season.☀️
Some of our events will stay in the calendar, but there may be times when our ride leaders won’t be there. If that happens, we’d really appreciate it if you could still ride according to the plan of the event.
Our ZTPL Cycling Club events will be back in autumn 2026.
For now — RIDE ON, enjoy the good weather, and if you can’t, just have a great time at our events.

We invite you to a joint event with ZTPL Cycling Club and our partner WINTERTAL, a manufacturer of casual clothing for cyclists.
◾️You can find our collection at: https://wintertal.com/en/133-ztplcc

Our Friday "100" will help you learn to ride for a long time at specific weight/kg values.
The pace will be 99% equal, but ultimately it is up to the leaders whether there will be any accents, e.g. on the climbs. The given assumptions in w/kg refer to the average value of the entire trip.

◾️Group D is a beginner group with distance 50km only. The pace is calm, we learn how to ride in a Zwift peleton with avg pace 2-2.6 w/kg.
◾️Group C is between group D and more advanced Group B. Pace 2.6-3.0 w/kg.
Important: If your FTP is around 2.8w/kg, this group will not be for you
◾️Group B is designed for more advanced users. The pace will oscillate around 3.0-3.5w/kg. If the leader finds it necessary to ride stronger, be prepared for some intervals!
Important: If your FTP is around 3.2w/kg, this group will not be for you

◾️Please listen to the leader’s commands which are displayed in yellow during the whole ride. The leader who leads the group is highlighted in yellow. Please don’t pass the leader.
◾️Please follow the leader instructions! Especially if the leader notices that the group has been swept away. The leader is highlighted in yellow.
◾️At the and wait for the leader’s command: GOGOGO and start your sprint. Enjoy!

◾️If you would like to get yourself our ZTPL Cycling Club team jersey please visit our shop https://inoni.cc/en/?s=ztpl&post_type=product
or you can order our Casual T-Shirt and Hoodies https://wintertal.com/en/133-ztplcc

ZTPL Cycling Club 100km ride with Wintertal (E)

name: BST Mountaingoat Race
description:
The race is for everyone who wants a good, high quality workout and competition with likeminded people. Remember to pace yourself, there will
be climbs to conquer. The race should be as fair as possible so the following rules apply. Racers on zPower, lacking heart rate monitors, riding in an inappropriate category, or riding very unrealistically will be disqualified. Powerups are turned off as well. 

Final results will be available on zwiftpower.com. So get your bottles and turn on your fan, it's race time! 

When you sign up please select the correct category below (your FTP divided by your weight in kg):
(A) 4.0+ W/kg FTP
(B) 3.2 - 3.99 W/kg FTP
(C) 2.5 - 3.19 W/kg FTP
(D) 0 - 2.49 W/kg FTP
(E) Women Only

name: INC RELENTLESS Sprint Series Race 2
description:
Welcome to the RELENTLESS sprint race series Race 2
Shorter compact races normally around 10km per race and 2 races per race day.
Race 1 normally a hill (Waitopia Hilly, Innsbruck etc)
Race 2 normally flat (Dolphin, Bell, CCW)
Designed to be high intensity for short amounts of time.
Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at http://www.zwiftpower.com/ http://www.zwiftpower.comhttp://www.zwiftpower.com/

eventSubgroups.name: INC RELENTLESS Sprint Series Race 2 (A)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to the RELENTLESS sprint race series Race 2
Shorter compact races normally around 10km per race and 2 races per race day.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: CIS - Friday Sub 2.0 Social T.G.I.F Ride
description:
Celebrate and unwind as we head into the weekend with a Sub 2.0 CIS Social Group Ride with Coach David, Head Coach at CIS Training Systems.  

About CIS Training Systems: As an early adaptor, CIS became one the first Cycling Training and Coaching Service Providers to enter the virtual space on Zwift back in early September 2014. CIS is an inclusive community of cyclists that share common goals and work together to achieve them under the guidance of experienced and knowledgeable coaches.

Learn how to Train with Purpose and discover a whole community of cyclists to do it with. For more information contact us at: http://www.ciscyclingnyc.com/index.html

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

name: Castelli Race Series
description:
Welcome to the Castelli Race Series! 

Race 5 of 5.

Each season comprises 5 weeks with a race each Friday at 18:20 CET. Your best 4 results count toward final standings each season. 

Open to all, with the aim of having a little fun while increasing fitness.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at <http://www.zwiftpower.com/> http://www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: 3R PedalPlay Interval Ride [~3.3-3.7w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

#3r

name: SZR Funride
description:
Welcome to the SZR (Swedish Zwift Riders) “Funride”.

During the “Winter” (September to March) the group will be led by Neil the “Meanest Ride Leader” on Zwift.
His job is to break you, hurt you!..... DO NOT LET THIS HAPPEN!
If you finish this ride you can look yourself in the mirror after and say I kicked butt!

During the “Summer” (April to August) the group will be led by Greasy Pig, he is no way near as mean as Neil, but he does like the odd climb or two.
The ride may have fence sprints, this is where the lead will pull the fence in to 2 seconds, then push the fence out to 10 seconds, then you can use the fence as a point to sprint to, then regroup on the leader after.

Use our discord if you like to have a chat with the people on the ride
https://discord.gg/DQHybQT

#szr

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

description:

name: Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride
description:
WELCOME TO ALL RIDERS!
Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride is not a fast pace group ride or race.

RULES:
Please join us for our event for a pace between (1.8- 2.2 w/kg) for 60 mins.
The focus of the group ride is keeping the group together the entire ride.
Follow the ride Leader (yellow beacon) instructions at all times, the fence is the killer one, so if you don't respect the leader pace, it will kick you out, our advice is to join the sweeper behind the group if you have extra watts.

Staying in the group draft is very important.
Your jersey will be autoselected on the Zwift Christmas Kit .
Please, add tag [ITA] after your name.

SPRINTS:
Regroup right after optional sprints.
This is the Facebook group of Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly/
and Strava Club of Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
Join us! Have fun! Ride On!

-
BENVENUTO A TUTTI I RIDERS!
Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride non è una gara ma una pedalata di gruppo.

REGOLE:
Unitevi a noi per il nostro evento per un ritmo tra 1.8- 2.2w/kg per 60 minuti.
L'obiettivo della pedalata è mantenere il gruppo unito per tutta la durata dell'evento.
Segui sempre le istruzioni del Leader (segnale giallo), se andrete oltre la barriera rossa vuol dire che non state rispettando il passo del leader e verrete espulsi, il nostro consiglio è di aiutare lo sweeper, con la freccia rossa, se avete più ritmo.

Rimanere nella pancia di gruppo è molto importante, la scia è molto forte.
La tua maglia verrà autoselezionata sul kit Natalizio di Zwift.
Per favore, aggiungi il tag [ITA] dopo il tuo nome.

SPRINT:
attendere il Leader subito dopo gli sprint, che sono facoltativi.

Questo è il gruppo Facebook del Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly/
Strava Club del Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy.

description:

name: TrainerRoad Throwdown Series
description:
Push your limits and get faster with the TrainerRoad Throwdown Series!

//What it Is
The TrainerRoad Throwdown Series is your chance to test your training, push your limits, and challenge yourself on fresh courses each week.
Each race is short, hard, and built to fit into your week—perfect for sharpening fitness and fueling your motivation.

//Rules
- You must have a ZwiftPower.com account in order to be eligible for prizes.
- Zwift Racing Score used for seeding..
- You must use a power meter (zPower not allowed) and heart rate monitor during the race.
- No TT bikes allowed.
- Only 1 Power-up will be granted at the beginning of the race.

//Eligibility
- Participants must have a valid ZwiftPower account and follow all race rules to be included in official results.

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Team Evolve)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Team Evolve (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/96ff10e9-461d-40b1-b62d-24abcacd202f).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: 3R Racing
description:
This RACE is brought to you by 3R to help draw attention to our charity partners. One example is how Buffalo Bikes (provided by the charity WBR for £100/each) can benefit the world's transport needy families.
Expect this RACE to be highly competitive across all CATs. In order to make the racing as fair as possible, racers on zPower, lacking heart rate monitors, riding an an inappropriate category, or riding very unrealistically may be disqualified.
Final results will be available on zwiftpower.com. We hope that everyone gets a great workout and has some good fun in this part of 3R’s #prostyletraining offering. #rideon #RideRaceRaise

name: PUMP: Loop Logic V — Friday (5 Laps)
description:
Same Distance. More Laps. Maximum Sass. ♥️🌶️

Welcome to Loop Logic, the weekday series where we prove that "going in circles" is actually a superpower. We’ve curated a week of routes that hit the 19–25km sweet spot with enough climbing to keep your glutes talking back to you.

The Logic is Simple:
We start with one big loop on Monday and add a lap every day until Friday. One lap is a warm-up, but five laps is a statement.

The PUMP Vibe:
Power in Numbers: No band here, ladies! This is your chance to find your rhythm, tuck into a friendly draft, and realize you’re much faster than you think.

Elevation is Our Love Language: Expect at least 100m of climb—just enough to feel the PUMP and earn that post-ride coffee.

Laughter > Watts: We ride hard, but we laugh harder. Whether you’re smashing a PR or just hanging onto a wheel for dear life, do it with a smile.

The Mission:
Show up for yourself, stay for the sisterhood, and let’s show exactly what women-powered logic looks like.

eventSubgroups.name: PUMP: Loop Logic V — Friday (5 Laps) (E)

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Foudre Friday Apero League Race
description:
🚴 Course Hebdomadaire Team Foudre

    Quand : Tous les Vendredis soirs.
    Heure de départ : 19h15 (France/CET), soit 18h15 (UK/GMT ou BST).
    Objectif : S'amuser et s'affronter.
    Format : Chaque semaine, un parcours différent.
    Ligue : Les courses comptent pour notre ligue du mois (composée de 4 ou 5 courses).
        Précision : Seules les 3 meilleures courses de chaque coureur seront retenues pour le classement mensuel de la ligue.

🏆 Classements et Points (Course au Scratch)

    Classements : Un classement individuel et un classement par équipe.
    Système de Points (Arrivée) :
        1er : 100 points
        2e : 99 points
        3e : 98 points
        ... et ainsi de suite jusque 1 point.
    Période ZRL : Des reconnaissances des parcours sont organisées durant la période ZRL 

🔗 Comment nous rejoindre et obtenir plus d'infos

    Planning Mensuel : Sur nos pages Facebook :
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/foudre.friday.apero.league
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/180845029459880
    Toutes les Infos Utiles : Via notre serveur Discord, notamment dans les salons publics de l'équipe :
        https://discord.gg/YzcVYJY



Et en anglais:




🚴 Team Foudre's Friday Night Race
Team Foudre invites you to come have fun and compete in our Friday night race! ⚡️
Here is a summary of the key points for our event:
📅 Weekly Race Details

    When: Every Friday night.
    Start Time: 7:15 PM (France/CET), which is 6:15 PM (UK/GMT or BST).
    Goal: To have fun and compete.
    Format: A different course every week.
    League: The races count towards our monthly league (consisting of 4 or 5 races).
        Important Note: Only the best 3 races of each rider will be retained for the monthly league ranking.

🏆 Standings and Points (Scratch Race)

    Standings: An individual ranking and a team ranking.
    Point System (Finish):
        1st: 100 points
        2nd: 99 points
        3rd: 98 points
        ... and so on down to 1 point.
    ZRL Period: Course recons (reconnaissance rides) are organized during the ZRL period.

🔗 How to Join Us and Get More Info

    Monthly Schedule: On our Facebook pages:
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/foudre.friday.apero.league
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/180845029459880
    All Useful Info: Via our Discord server, particularly in the team's public channels:
        https://discord.gg/YzcVYJY

name: PACK🐺 1.5 Weekly Wind Down
description:
This PACK 1.5 beginner friendly event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience at an average lap pace of around 1.3-1.5w/kg.

On the flat, the pace will be controlled to ensure the group draft effect is kept under tight control with a target of 1.5w/kg on any hills or upslopes. The Leader will text instructions as well as use discord voice chat as communication is the key to a good group ride. Sweepers will also be in operation to assist in helping the group stay together as this is a non-drop ride.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/pack

https://discord.gg/cce7ers

https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: ZZRC The Weekend Starts Here
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Weekend Starts Here
2-2.5.This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2-2.5 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be as advertised.

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicate a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel
https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: GXY SHOOTING STARS [2.8-3.2 wkg] CAT C
description:
You are blasting your way through the Zwift Cosmos at around 3.0 w/kg for 75 minutes – right at the top end of Cat C.

This ride is designed for strong C-riders who are comfortable riding hard and holding wheels.

The mission: keep the Shooting Star intact as we travel through the Galaxy.

⚠️ This is an unswept ride – there are no sweepers. Once you lose the Meteor’s tail, space gets very cold very fast. Ride smart, stay together and help each other keep the group intact.

On the climbs, expect short warp-speed surges up to ~4.0 w/kg to keep the group moving and avoid splits.

Let’s have some fun, stay in formation and enjoy the ride as we blast through the Galaxy.

(Power suggestions based on a 75kgs rider.)

If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link and clubs feature,

or by the email below:

https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/

https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894

https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY SHOOTING STARS [2.8-3.2 wkg] CAT C (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
You are blasting our way through the Zwift Cosmos at somewhere around 3.0 wkg for 60 mins.
You will join other like minded riders whose aim will be to keep the Shooting Star in tact.
Anyone falling off the Meteor’s tail will wherever possible be brought back into the purple cigar shaped Meteoroid.
Let’s have some fun, riding off into the Galaxy before we crash back down to Earth after 60 mins just in time to get the weekend started!

name: Het is Koers Weekend Starter !
description:
Het is Koers, the famous Dutch cycling blog, presents you a variety of well guided rides. On Friday evening we make a party to start the weekend.

Weekends starter (C, 2.3-2.8W/kg)
Between 1st Sunday of October until the last Sunday of March, we will offer you a C (2.3 - 2.8w/kg) category paced social but spicy ride. Your ride leader completes the ride with some sprints and/or climbs.
All riders are encouraged to keep the bunch together, so we can start and finish as one happy blob. Everybody is welcome as long as you make a positive contribution in making this a good and relaxed start of the weekend.
Please have a nice chat and ride, give your fellow riders a Ride On and share your beers/drinks after the ride on our HIK Facebook Group page.
Between 1st Sunday of April until last Sunday of September HIK schedules the C category rides as a free ride (no lead) on challenging routes. On weekly basis you can ride with other C category riders to start your weekend.

Weekends starter (D, 1.7-2.2W/kg)
All year round HIK schedules the D category rides as a free ride (so no lead) on mainly flat routes. On weekly basis you can ride with other D category riders to start your weekend.

Check out our weekly ride announcements on the HIK Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/hetiskoers
Join Het Is Koers on Zwift https://www.zwift.com/clubs/HetIsKoers/home
Other HIK events:
Endurance Recovery! (C) 2.2-2.6 W/kg, Saturday 9.00 AM CET
In the Dutch mountains (C) 2.5-3.0 W/kg, Sunday 9.00 AM CET
Watts More! (C) 2.5-3.0 W/kg, Tuesday 19.00 CET
Something Different! (D) 2.2-2.3 W/kg, Thursday 19.15 CET

name: Zwift Epic Race - Coastal Crown Loop
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: Friday Night Fun Race
description:
Get ready for the Friday Night Fun Race – the perfect way to unwind and kick-off your weekend! This is fast, friendly, and totally unserious racing designed for pure enjoyment.

Every week brings a different course and theme, so you never know what fun-fueled surprise awaits! And if that wasn't enough, everyone starts with a 'BOOST' power-up, with extras available throughout the race – that’s a speed increase for a few seconds – use it wisely!

SCHEDULE
Races are held weekly on Friday evenings across various timezones.

CATEGORIES
You’ll be categorized by your existing Racing Score for fair competition. But don't worry – race results won't impact your score, keeping the pressure low and the fun high.

Sign up for the ultimate Friday night race – fun-filled, feel-good, and guaranteed to make your weekend start with a bang!

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: ZZRC Goat Airlines Flight Sub 2
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Goat Airlines Sub 2. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.7-1.9 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be sub 2 .

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel for our sub 2
https://discord.gg/RJ3dhbjCEp


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW (C)

name: Friday Fun - GTR Group Ride
description: Start your weekend with a friendly, 1-hour group ride with the Good Time Riders, team GTR. Get the legs spinning for an hour and work off a few calories before you hit the weekend hard. Our yellow ride leaders will aim to ride between 2.0 and 2.3 w/kg, with our red beacons helping to keep the group together at the rear.The fence will be switched on in front of the ride leader to encourge group riding at the advertised pace.

name: Flash Race - Team INOX
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍💨 Flash Race by Team INOX ⚡️🚴🏼‍💨
• You must have an active Zwift Power account on https://zwiftpower.com
• Heart rate monitors are mandatory for podium positions
• Smart trainers or power meters must be connected as the power source to participate in races
Make sure you meet all requirements to avoid disqualification

The Flash Race is an intensely competitive race on short route, tests pure cyclist power. Time trial bikes are allowed, enabling athletes to maximize aerodynamics.

Do you have what it takes to run a mile faster than FLASH? Test yourself 
We’re waiting for you every Friday. Daily results on Zwiftpower.

More info will be communicated promptly via the Discord and Facebook channels
The organizers reserve the right to carry out checks in case of suspected misconduct and if necessary cancel the performances obtained

CONTACT US
• Website: https://www.teaminox.it/
• Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/inoxteam/
• Zwift Companion Club: https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/cef70cde...44703/home
• Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cpr3rBGaZy
• ZwiftPower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=16461
• STRAVA: https://strava.app.link/p2Oj6TaE3Gb

Everyone is Welcome! 🙏🏻

name: EVOLVE 25km Road Race
description:
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 25, an electrifying 25km race on Zwift, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair
atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply
staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: WCC Friday Fried Legs Recovery Ride
description:
Welcome to the WCC Friday Fried Legs Recovery ride. Did you find you burnt all your matches on the TTT yesterday? Do you need a relaxing social recovery ride to work out the kinks that remain? Just looking for an enjoyable 60 minute chin wag? You have found the right place. 
With our well trained Leaders and a sweep team to boot we will spin at a relaxed 1.3-1.5wkg where you will find the hour quickly passing.

Looking to join WCC? We can be found at any of the following links…

Via Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/groups/2665592713734072/?ref=share 

website
https://www.wobblecycleclub.com

Or via the Club on the ZC app
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/wcc/home

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Sledgehammer Inbetweeners Races
description:
Welcome to the Sledgehammer series.
These races are designed to equalise the categories as much as possible and encourage team riding between riders of different strengths. Please enter your appropriate category according to your Zwiftpower race category.
All out of category entrants will be excluded from the Zwiftpower results.
Organiser's Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2388754431339748/
Race, steer, draft. Team up and chase those faster riders!

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
description:
No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
No Spring Chickens is back for another round of masters racing! This is your chance to compare
yourself to riders that are the same age. It doesn’t matter if you have passed the spring chicken
stage only recently or a little longer ago, this is a race for you.
No Spring Chickens is a mass start race, all age groups will have their own starting pen, but they will
all take off together. After the finish the results for men and women will be split on ZwiftPower. That
way we hope everyone will find someone to race with and have some fun.
Series schedule:
20-22 March: Wandering Flats (25.2 km, flat, Makuri)
27-29 March: Green to Screen (28.5 km, rolling, New York)
3-5 April: Power Punches (23.7 km, repeated small climbs, Watopia)
10-12 April: Bon Voyage (31.4 km, flat to rolling, France)
17-19 April: Cobbled Crowns (24.3 km, short sharp hills, Richmond)
24-26 April:Itza Climb Finish (30 km, medium hilly finish, watopia)
Race rules:
1. No TT Bikes.
2. Steering is enabled.
3. Smarttrainer or powermeter required.
4. Heart rate monitor required.
5. No indoor bikes without controlled resistance (cadence=power).
6. Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results.
7. For podium finishers in all age groups: Elite level performances above the limits stated below
must be supported by dual power recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower
○ Men: above 5w/kg or 375w for 20min / 6w/kg or 475w for 5min / 9w/kg for 1min.
○ Women: above 4.5w/kg or 280w for 20min / 5.5w/kg or 350w for 5min / 7.5w/kg for
1min.

8. For podium finishers in all age groups: Zwift activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.

name: Group Run 10k
description:
Join us for a 10k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends. #pbt

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Velos Monuments – Amstel Gold Race – Spring Classics competition #8/12
description:
Following last year’s highly successful edition, we’re proud to launch the Spring Classics Competition 2026!
This 12 week series kicks off on 27 February 2026, with a race every Friday at 19:25 UTC.

Our format features:
• Mass start racing with separate standings per race category
• A time based general classification across all 12 races
• The 5 Monuments races, each counting towards a dedicated points classification
Whether you're chasing the podium, improving your fitness, or riding for fun with friends, the Velos community welcomes all motivated Zwifters.
Join us on the virtual roads and experience the thrill of the Spring Classics — Velos style!

Race 8/12
The Amstel Gold Race is situated in the hilliest part of the Netherlands near the tripoint with Belgium and Germany.
The race is hilly and packs a lot more elevation than you might think with climbs up to 15% and more.

Choice for this race would be Yorkshire Double Loop, covering its climbs in both directions.
In the end however, you will be surprised by the amount of elevation you have just conquered.

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Fast Friday Workout
description:
Congratulations, you crushed another week of workouts! If you’re looking to cap off a great week with another can't-miss training session, check out Fast Friday on Zwift. 

A new workout series to put in your weekly run rotation, Fast Friday is a simple and streamlined way to get your heart rate up. We’re focusing on short reps here, clocking in fast intervals that help runners increase their top speed. Short. Sharp. Fast. High-intensity intervals are proven to take your performance to the next level. So lace up and get moving for just 20–30 minutes every Friday before checking out of the office and into the weekend. 

#fastfriday

description:

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

name: Friday Night Fun Race
description:
Get ready for the Friday Night Fun Race – the perfect way to unwind and kick-off your weekend! This is fast, friendly, and totally unserious racing designed for pure enjoyment.

Every week brings a different course and theme, so you never know what fun-fueled surprise awaits! And if that wasn't enough, everyone starts with a 'BOOST' power-up, with extras available throughout the race – that’s a speed increase for a few seconds – use it wisely!

SCHEDULE
Races are held weekly on Friday evenings across various timezones.

CATEGORIES
You’ll be categorized by your existing Racing Score for fair competition. But don't worry – race results won't impact your score, keeping the pressure low and the fun high.

Sign up for the ultimate Friday night race – fun-filled, feel-good, and guaranteed to make your weekend start with a bang!

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Newbury Velo Crit
description:
Newbury Velo presents your weekly nocturnal criterium. No crashes. No marshals, No sign-on. No fee. Just 12 laps of Type2Fun.

Club results will be posted on our Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/newburyvelo

Riders must be registered on https://www.zwiftpower.com and have their Zwift profile agreed to share with zwiftpower otherwise they will not be included in the results. Think of it as the signing in process :) details of how to do this can be found here: https://boltrace.team/zwift-power-guide

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-5.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5 /kg-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg

#newburyvelo

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Full Glaz Challenge
description:
Le B&B HOTELS p/b KTM renoue avec la compétition sur Zwift. Affrontez-vous, tous les vendredis soirs, sur dix courses virtuelles dans les mondes de Watopia, Richmond, New-York, Londres, Innsbruck, France, Yorkshire, Bologne et Paris pour décrocher la victoire. Glazic sera parmi vous pour repérer les talents ! 

The Full Glaz Challenge is back. A set of 10 races organized by the Men in Glaz and open to all Zwifters! Compete against riders from all over the World.

name: VirtuSlo 4Endurance Steady Ride
description:
Welcome to our weekly VirtuSlo 4Endurance Steady Ride. 


This is a fun social ride with the aim of improving your endurance. Stick together to enjoy the draft and companionship. Feel free to join in-ride conversation.


We will not tolerate flyers/racers - they will be removed from the ride by the one and only mrs. Fence. 

We will try to have a sweeper on each ride to help any riders with technical (or other) difficulties come back to the group. If you feel strong, please help our red beacon team. We recommend using a »late-join« option if you fall behind in the first 30 minutes of the ride.


Feel free to join all our events at https://www.zwift.com/eu/events/tag/virtuslo
 
VirtuSlo 4Endurance Schedule
More info: https://zwiftpower.com/series.php?id=VIRTUSLO


During the rides we use the group ride channel on our discord server https://discord.gg/x8CNEMRteS 
You are welcome to join for even more interaction. 


Check out our website https://virtuslo.cc/ for more info about us.


#VirtuSlo #4Endurance

name: WOW Course Time Attack Race on Cadex TT bike with Draft
description:
The WOW Zwift events are the fastest way to collect happy miles while meeting exceptional riders/ friends on ZWIFT. While you will be assigned the fastest TT bike (Cadex) and the latest DT Swiss Disc wheel set in draft mode, you will still be upgrading your bike of choice selected in your garage before starting the ride.

Our Ds leave the pen first and hold the group together within D power limits. Several minutes later C’s will start and keep a higher end C pace. Further several minutes later the A-B train starts unleashing their magic to make up the delay from the groups starting ahead. Can all 3 teams come together in the final segments of the ride and celebrate the ONE WOW FAMILY? All groups will see each other, transfers between the groups on the go will be possible. The ride leaders will help make those transitions smooth.

WOW is a no drama, full fun ride!

Double draft is on and the Cadex TT bikes will be drafting!

name: Giant Friday Funday
description:
Join some of your favorite Giant leaders for a fun, social group ride with a pace that matches the description.
It will be a great introduction to group riding with full pace instructions and group banter.
The ride will target the 1.8 to 2.2 wkg average for the entire ride. Ride pace may occasionally be higher on uphills and lower on downhills but will average within the range.
Please ride with the beacon the entire time and have fun!
