name: Mini Little Tour Ghost Team – Etape 3
description:
Mini Little tour tout les semaines - meme Power UP - meme horaire.
* NEW TEXTE*: #MLTGT Pourquoi ne pas pimenter un peu notre calendrier et booster la participation aux événements ? Alors voilà : place au Mini Little Tour ! Un format court, intense, accessible et surtout ultra fun — pile ce qu’on aime tous sur Zwift. Le concept : Toutes les semaine 3 courses enchaînées, Little Tour Ghost Team… mais version express Courses courtes, dynamiques, accessibles à tous L’objectif : du rythme, de la bagarre, du plaisir. Horaire : Course 1 : 10h20 Course 2 : 10h50 Course 3 : 11h25 En 1h top chrono, tout est plié ! Pour être classé au général, il faut participer aux 3 courses à la suite. Barème de points par course : 1e : 100 pts 2e : 97 pts 3e : 94 pts 4e : 92 pts 5e : 90 pts 6e : 88 pts 7e : 86 pts 8e : 84 pts 9e : 82 pts 10e : 80 pts Puis de la 11e place on descend de 1 point par place (jusqu’à 0) En résumé : Nom : Mini Little Tour Fréquence : Tout les samedis matins Durée : 1h – 3 courses de 15 à 20 minutes chacune Objectif : dynamiser la participation, enrichir le classement maillot, et surtout… se faire plaisir ! 🔗 width=22 Lien du prochain Mini Little Tour Ghost Team 👉 width=22
https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/mltgt ou consultez notre https://www.zwifteur.com/ghost-team/calendrier-evenements

Why not spice up our calendar and boost event participation? So here it is: make way for the Mini Little Tour! A short, intense, accessible, and above all, ultra-fun format—exactly what we all love on Zwift.

The Concept: Every week:

3 back-to-back races, Little Tour Ghost Team style… but the express version.

Short, dynamic races, open to everyone.

The Goal: High pace, good battles, and pure fun.

Schedule:

Race 1: 10:20

Race 2: 10:50

Race 3: 11:25

All wrapped up in just 1 hour flat! To be ranked in the General Classification, you must participate in all 3 consecutive races.

Points Scale per Race:

1st: 100 pts

2nd: 97 pts

3rd: 94 pts

4th: 92 pts

5th: 90 pts

6th: 88 pts

7th: 86 pts

8th: 84 pts

9th: 82 pts

10th: 80 pts

Then, from 11th place onwards, points decrease by 1 point per place (down to 0).

In Summary:

Name: Mini Little Tour

Frequency: Every Saturday morning

Duration: 1h – 3 races of 15 to 20 minutes each

Goal: Boost participation, enrich the jersey classification, and above all… have fun!

Link to the next Mini Little Tour Ghost Team: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/mltgt Or check our calendar: https://www.zwifteur.com/ghost-team/calendrier-evenements

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: [ITALY RUNNERS] - The 15km Challenge Test 🚀
description:
🇬🇧 Fast 15km! Challenge yourself every Saturday!
🇮🇹 15km veloci! La tua sfida, ogni sabato.
🇩🇪 Schnelle 15km! Deine Challenge, jeden Samstag.
🇫🇷 15km rapides ! Ton défi, chaque samedi.
🇪🇸 ¡15km rápidos! Tu desafío, cada sábado.

🇬🇧 Ready for the test? Join the ITALY RUNNERS club and push your limits with us! 🚀 One Community. Your Pace.

🇮🇹 Pronto per la prova? Unisciti al club ITALY RUNNERS e spingi oltre il tuo limite con noi! 🚀 Una Community. Il Tuo Ritmo.

🇩🇪 Bereit für den Test? Tritt dem ITALY RUNNERS Club bei und geh mit uns an deine Grenzen! 🚀 Eine Gemeinschaft. Dein Tempo.

🇫🇷 Prêt pour le test ? Rejoins le club ITALY RUNNERS et dépasse-toi avec nous ! 🚀 Une Communauté. Ton Rythme.

🇪🇸 ¿Listo para la prueba? ¡Únete al club ITALY RUNNERS y supera tus límites con nosotros! 🚀 Una Comunidad. Tu Ritmo.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: DWGZ Hot Hundo – Get Gritty!
description:
DWGZ Hot Hundo – Get Gritty!

Welcome to DWGZ (Determined Whitty Gritty Zwifters) — a global community of cyclists who thrive on shared suffering, doing hard stuff, lifting people up, sharing good energy, and building a strong worldwide crew of gritty riders.

Our weekly 100 Mile (160.9 KM) Hot Hundo Endurance Ride is built for those who live for long efforts, steady watts, and that sweet mix of camaraderie and challenge. Whether you’re chasing endurance goals or just want to test your limits alongside an awesome group, the Hot Hundo delivers.

Ride Categories:

🚴A Category – 3.9+ w/kg (averaging in the low to mid 4s)

🚴B Category – 3.2–3.8 w/kg

🚴C Category – 2.8–3.2 w/kg

🚴D Category – 1.6–2.5 w/kg

Expect consistent pacing, a spicy but supportive vibe, and a crew of riders who show up, dig deep, and finish strong. Whether you’re rolling with the beacon or hammering with the front, you’ll find your tribe here.

At DWGZ, we ride six days a week at 6:05AM Eastern (US) — and every ride is an opportunity to build strength, connection, and a little grit.

So clip in, hang on, and bring the good watts.

DWGZ Hot Hundo — where endurance, community, and passion come together. Get Gritty! 🔥🚴‍♂️

name: RollCo: Rolling Tide - 11K
description:
🌊 ROLLING TIDE
Start small. Build big.
Welcome to Rolling Tide — RollCo’s season-long ritual race.

Every week, the distance grows by 1 km.
It begins as a short, explosive opener and steadily evolves into a full-distance endurance epic by year’s end.
This isn’t a one-off event — it’s a shared journey that rises with you.

🏁 Race Format
Rolling Tide is a categorised scratch race.
All riders start together, with categories keeping the racing fair and fast.
The only thing that changes… is the distance.

Week 1 → 1 km
Week 52 → 52 km

Early weeks reward raw power.
Later weeks demand patience, grit, and endurance.
The tide never stops rising.

Rolling Tide is about more than today’s finish line.
It’s about showing up — week after week.
Seeing the same names.
Measuring how far you’ve come as the distance stretches.

You’ll build rivalries.
You’ll track your progress.
You’ll remember the day it all began.

And by the end of the season, everyone who stayed will share the same truth:

“We rode the whole tide together.”

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: HangOn Ride by Team EN
description:
Join Endurance Nation for the Hang-On Ride -- a group ride that will truly test your limits! Warm up at 2.5 W/kg for 10’, then increase to 3.5 over 10’, then 3.5+ as you are able in your group. Your mission -- to HANG ON to the faster riders! Done right, C and D riders can work to stay in the groupetto as the Bs and As push the front and try to catch any fliers right at the end. This is not a race full of surges, but there is plenty of hard work to stay in the group. Casualties are inevitable, but come back and HANG ON longer next week! We will ride for about 60k, so come prepared with lots of fluids and food. Please pay close attention to leader's instructions via message.

Join TeamEN members to chat on an app called Discord. Discord is not required, but the ride experience is enhanced by using it. Click on this link for more information about Discord and related setup: https://www.endurancenation.us/teamen-on-zwift#discord

For more specifics about this ride and various other Endurance Nation rides, go to: https://www.endurancenation.us/teamen-on-zwift

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Run In The Park
description:
Come and join the original Zwift group run! 

This event is a 5km mass start event and runs at multiple times every Saturday.  

It is open to runners of all abilities so whether you are a recreational runner or an elite athlete you are welcome on the start line.  

Get to the start gates early to chat with the other runners, you may find someone of a similar ability to group up with for the run.  

There is no time limit for completing the event so run at your own pace and enjoy the course.

name: Endurance Group ride hosted by ZTBR- Zwift Team Brazil
description:
This weekly ride is designed for anyone interested in riding at your OWN endurance pace with a fun and welcoming group.

Event will have the ‘everyone stays together’ feature enabled. So you will stay with the group as long you keep pedaling.
You define your output according to your abilities and mood being sure you won’t be dropped.

We also have 30 min late ticket enabled.
Pedal for as long as you want.

The event is normally 120min long, but sometimes it can be longer or shorter to cover a specific Zwift route.

Join us on Instagram @zwiftteambrazil [website] ztbr.com.br

#ztbr #z2

name: FRR - Recce Races
description:
As we head towards our indoor season we are hosting 'Recce Races' so you have an opportunity to review and check courses coming for Tours, 6IX - race them, tempo together or simply explore - use the events as you need to - it's your time so use it well.

For all details and registration for our Tours or 6IX Team Racing then vist our website:
https://flammerougeracing.co.uk/

FRR Discord

Invite link: https://discord.com/invite/nQNWHQK6PS

name: GGCC Glasgow Green Saturday Training Ride
description:
GGCC group rides are TRAINING events, category C, with a yellow beacon leader. The beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average wherever possible.

Please look out for our training rides 3 days a week, every week - Tuesdays at 20.45 UTC, Thursdays at 21.00 UTC and Saturdays at 11.30am UTC. Use #ggcc to search, as in https://zwift.com/events/tag/ggcc

The Glasgow Green Cycle Club (GGCC) training rides are intended to provide a challenge for riders, and are open to all timezones. You should ride as a group, following the instructions of the beacon ride leader(s) for any sprints, intervals and spicy segments, regrouping after each when requested. All our group rides also have a closing minirace, to start from behind the beacon.

GGCC is a Glasgow based cycling club whose “in real life” (irl) club runs and training rides are always organized into pace groups, which match the Zwift categories quite well. GGCC also has a good race pedigree and presence in Scotland, and so we have organized this training ride series to suit what we believe is relevant to bike riders’ interests in performance and improvement in our club, in other Glasgow clubs, in Scotland and the UK. Racers will, no doubt, aspire to try our other events, both our Monday 20.10 UTC Zwift races, and our Friday 19.45 UTC TTs.

As we expect our club’s riders to be able to ride 50 miles at a 15mph average on our Sunday rides, such steady riding members will be comfortable with Zwift Category C, and in our various rides we cater for all abilities, through the Zwift ABCD levels. Beacon leaders communicate via Zwift messaging (always) and/or Discord (sometimes) to vary the pace to allow for sprints, intervals, and optionally a mini-race towards the end of the rides, with regrouping after each segment. Make as much use of drafting as you can, for more effective sprinting and intervals.

Results can be seen at the end of the ride on screen, and we will, in common with many other ride series, publish your results on zwiftpower.com too, based on each category.

All bikes and PUs are allowed, but better not to use TT bikes (which don’t draft) on our group rides and races. Please add GGCC (X) to your Zwift name to allow your ride presence to be recognized, where X is your desired ride category. Categories are related to your Functional Threshold Power (FTP); use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg, then pick the correct ride and group.

C: 2 W/kg to 3 W/kg FTP; the beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average.

PLEASE NOTE: Strava data for our rides must be open (not private or hidden). Please use a heart monitor so that we can monitor correlated power and heart rate to help assure fair riding.
You can use our Discord channel to talk to each other at https://discord.gg/UDhdEQA
Please see the GGCC Zwift page link at http://www.glasgowgreencycleclub.co.uk/ for more.

For more about specific Zwift courses for our events, see the Zwift Companion schedule, and also https://zwiftinsider.com/ where there is a wealth of information about all things Zwift.

#ggcc

name: EVOLVE 50km Road Race
description:
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 50, an electrifying 50km race on Zwift, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. 

This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. 

Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: Team Not Pogi - Saturday Endurance Spin
description:
Looking for a relaxed but motivational group ride?	
			
Join Team Not Pogi for our Endurance Spin - a social, steady paced ride designed to build fitness without the suffering!			
	
We'll roll together at a comfortable endurance pace (2.0 - 2.8 wkg), chat and enjoy the ride. No pressure just good vibes, smooth turns of the pedals, and a great way to stack some base miles with a supportive group.

eventSubgroups.name: Team Not Pogi - Saturday Endurance Spin (E)

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: MGCC Bagel
description:
The Morning Glory Cycling Club (MGCC) Bagel ride is a long standing Saturday tradition... not an easy ride, but a friendly don't get dropped type of ride. The Virtual Bagel will not be much different!
Group C: Warm up ~2.0 for 7 minutes. Rest of the ride will be at around 2.3-2.5/kg and the hills will be max at 3.2/kg.
There will be a sprint at the end for the last 700 metres.
Power UPs: Allowed / Bikes: No TT Allowed
Riders are encouraged to work as a group and to make thee a little more personal, please join the Voice Channel on Discord (all groups will be in the same channel):
Channel: https://discord.gg/K7vRDS3
Race results will be posted on http://www.zwiftpower.com/
For more info, please join the MGCC Zwift Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/323925517987763/

Disclaimer: MGCC Zwift rides are not official events sanction by MGCC - Morning Glory Cycling Club, or the OCA – Ontario Cycling Association. You participate at your own risk.

name: DIRT Dadurday Chase Race
description:
Race category starts will be staggered with the E category starting first, and each D, C, B, and A category starting later.

The riders will be challenged to work together using the draft, and group tactics in order to catch the category ahead of them while also holding off the categories that started after.

Races will have 3 power-ups enabled with equal odds (33% each):
draft, feather, & aero!
Compete with the best, and see how you stack up!

You must be opted-in on Zwift.com & ZwiftPower.com to be counted for race results.
We highly encourage A racers to follow Zwift eRacing Ruleset.
Dual recording of trainer & power meter, and recent weigh-in on ZwifTransparency
recommended--we reserve the right to ask for this information to verify outstanding
performances.

Categories are based on your Zwift Racing Score:
A: 800 - 1000
B: 600 - 800
C: 400 - 600
D: 200 - 400
E: 0 - 200

name: Pretzels, Epics and Afterparties p/b Velos
description:
April 🥨 RIDES

Each week in April, we'll be visiting a different route on Zwift, taking on one of the epic Zwift rides. This month we'll be throwing in routes with lots of climbs, some sprints and plenty of KOMs, should you choose to speed and regroup.

Although the ride officially stops at 2 hours, we strongly encourage you to carry on for the after party, and bag that route badge.

Pick your intensity wisely, 2.0w/kg (red beacon) will be zone 2 for many cat C riders, 2.5w/kg (yellow beacon) is zone 2 for moderate to high cat B riders (or choose to push your w/kg a bit higher by using a MTB).

The Velos jersey is unassigned during this event but you will unlock it if you complete the 2 hour ride, please wear our jersey to show your support if you have it in your garage🙏. Long rides can drag on your own, so jump in, and Team Velos will help the time fly past with the friendly chat and questionable jokes. Don't forget to grab a bidon or two, and bring some snacks!

SCHEDULE

4th April -Three Sisters -48 km (29.8 miles), 897m (2943’) of elevation; We will tackle the Hilly KOM, EPIC KOM, and the Volcano KOM.
( 1:45-2:00)

11th April - Big Foot Hills- 67.5 km (41.9 miles), 708m (2,323’) of elevation; A trip through titans grove and a climb up the Zwift Kim and Volcano (1:50-2:15)

18th April - Sacre Bleu- 71.2 km (44.2 miles), with 396 m (1,299’) elevation loads of sprint chances!(2:10 -2:20).

25th April - Zwift Games Epic 79.6 km (49.5miles) 821m (2694’) elevation; plenty of sprint opportunities and a few small KOMs(2:20-2:45)

Join our community on FB https://www.facebook.com/groups/366062821803204
Discord Channel. https://discord.gg/TDBE3FA

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (DIRT)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by DIRT (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7c48063-540e-44ce-8ae5-686ebc64db05).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
description:
No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
No Spring Chickens is back for another round of masters racing! This is your chance to compare
yourself to riders that are the same age. It doesn’t matter if you have passed the spring chicken
stage only recently or a little longer ago, this is a race for you.
No Spring Chickens is a mass start race, all age groups will have their own starting pen, but they will
all take off together. After the finish the results for men and women will be split on ZwiftPower. That
way we hope everyone will find someone to race with and have some fun.
Series schedule:
20-22 March: Wandering Flats (25.2 km, flat, Makuri)
27-29 March: Green to Screen (28.5 km, rolling, New York)
3-5 April: Power Punches (23.7 km, repeated small climbs, Watopia)
10-12 April: Bon Voyage (31.4 km, flat to rolling, France)
17-19 April: Cobbled Crowns (24.3 km, short sharp hills, Richmond)
24-26 April:Itza Climb Finish (30 km, medium hilly finish, watopia)
Race rules:
1. No TT Bikes.
2. Steering is enabled.
3. Smarttrainer or powermeter required.
4. Heart rate monitor required.
5. No indoor bikes without controlled resistance (cadence=power).
6. Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results.
7. For podium finishers in all age groups: Elite level performances above the limits stated below
must be supported by dual power recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower
○ Men: above 5w/kg or 375w for 20min / 6w/kg or 475w for 5min / 9w/kg for 1min.
○ Women: above 4.5w/kg or 280w for 20min / 5.5w/kg or 350w for 5min / 7.5w/kg for
1min.

8. For podium finishers in all age groups: Zwift activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

This event has “rubberbanding” enabled, so you will stay with the group as long as you keep pedaling. Feel free to go as hard or as easy as you like for the duration.

name: 3R Racing
description:

Final results will be available on zwiftpower.com. We hope that everyone gets a great workout and has some good fun in this part of 3R’s #prostyletraining offering. #rideon #RideRaceRaise

description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

description:
Let's warm up and enjoy riding together as one group before today's BMTR Flat 100 (Miles) event, which has three categories: B, C, and D. The event starts in 35 minutes.

Since this is a 'banded' event, everyone can warm up at their own pace while the Zwift 'Rubberband' feature keeps us together.

While there aren't any official Yellow or Red Beacons for this event, you'll see familiar BMTR Ride Leaders and Sweeps joining you for some warm-up fun!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Atom Racing Team DD Race
description:
Welcome to the Atom Racing Team’s double draft race. All riders will start at the same time and to give a larger bunch the best chance of riding together we have added double draft. Get out the blocks fast and try to hold on to the fastest group you can. Use the draft to hold on to riders who are stronger than you normally. We will always use nice flat courses to race on but beware we may soometimes use event finishes based on distance and not traditional lap endings.
Race it or take it easy, its your choice. Either way, you’ll end the ride with the prize of a shiny new Atom Racing Team in game kit for your garage.
If you like what you see, come and join us in our Zwift club or join us for real by searching for our Facebook group.

name: SROZ's Ride 'n Shine
description:
The Striders and Riders of Zwift's “Ride ‘n Shine” is a social ride where community comes first.

We pride ourselves on a friendly, respectful, and supportive atmosphere...perfect for those looking for a great workout and even better company.

The terrain might get a little spicy at times, but we aim for that sweet-spot average of 18–20 mph (30–32 kph).

Stick with the leader’s peloton! We’re better together...plus, you’ll save a ton of energy by tucking into that group draft.

Feeling a little gapped? Don't sweat it. We strive to have a dedicated sweeper on every ride to help pull you back into the fold.

The "Gene Autry" Philosophy: We believe in freedom! As the legend himself said, we won’t fence you in.

At the end of the day, you’re the captain of your own bike. Whether you’re pushing the pace or soaking in the scenery: ride your ride!

So come out, enjoy the virtual fresh air, and keep the faith!

eventSubgroups.name: SROZ's Ride 'n Shine  (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
The Striders and Riders “Ride ‘n Shine” Casual, Social Ride is a group ride in a friendly, supportive, respectful, and enjoyable atmosphere. 🎉🚴🏻‍♂️😁

The pace may vary depending on the course, but we strive to ride and socialize with an overall average pace of approximately 18-20mph (30-32kph).  

Please be prepared to ride with the group leader’s peloton, so everyone benefits from the group draft effect. 

We will try to have a sweeper on each ride to help any rider who may want to return to the group. 

Remember, the choice is yours…ride your ride!

So, enjoy your ride, keep the faith, and become that someone!

name: Moxie Ride
description: Moxie Ride is a progressive w/k based ride. Increased effort every 30 minutes by .25 w/k, with the first 30 minutes at 2.25w/k. The ride is 2hours.

name: GC Coaching's Amalgam
description:
GC Coaching's structured group workouts are geared towards athletes in
their base phase, so if you are looking for a Hammerfest, this isn't your
ride. These workouts are focused on building aerobic capacity and will
consist of mainly zone 2-4 riding, with some zone 5 sprinkled in at times
for good measure. We will also be mixing up the power, cadence, and
position on the bike throughout the course of the workout further challenge
your basic skills and on-bike flexibility. Come and join us!

*BIO*:
Shayne holds a bachelors degree in Health Science in Professional
Development and Advanced Patient Care, is a licensed physical therapy
assistant in Massachusetts, a USA Cycling Level 1 (expert level) certified
Coach, a USA Cycling Power Based Training Certified Coach, a Precision
Nutrition Level 1 Certified Coach, a US Military Endurance Sports (USMES)
affiliated Coach, and USA Olympic Committee Safe Sport certified.

He is the owner and head Coach of GC Coaching, Workout Content Editor at
Zwift, the creator of P2 Coached Computraining, and the creator of Zwift’s
“Build Me Up”, "Pebble Pounder", and “201: Your First 5k” Flexible Training
Plans. He has also been involved with Zwift Academy since 2019.

As an award-winning author, he has been published in Bicycling Magazine,
the TrainingPeaks blog, Zwift Insider, and hosts the Never Going Pro
podcast.

He can be reached directly through the GC Coaching website for questions
and coaching inquiries: https://gccoaching.fit/

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: Endurance Odyssey - Power2Tri Endurance Coaching
description:
TODAY’S SESSION
Saturday sessions will largely be focused on developing the aerobic engine to support the upcoming race season.  While the intensity will generally be fairly low we will, on occasion throw some higher intensity efforts in, but overall intensity (IF) will be kept to .80 or lower.   Please note the Ride Leader generally rides outdoors on Saturdays, but if conditions don’t allow he’ll be joining you.  

ABOUT ONGOING TRAINING
The 2024 Triathlon Season is generally over for most.  Until early Spring our focus will mostly be on form/efficiency, maintenance, and prep for the upcoming season.  And, if you’ve ridden with us before, you know I’ll likely be mixing in some punchy efforts

WORKOUT DESIGNER
Dave D'Abrosca, owner and head coach of Power2Tri Endurance Coaching, creates all of these workouts and will be leading the vast majority of them. Dave is a USAT Level II Long Course and Ironman Certified Coach with experience guiding athletes through hundreds of races, from first-timers to seasoned long course triathletes, as well as XTERRA, Gravel, MTB and Runners up to Ultra distance. Dave & Team are available for questions via www.power2tri-multisport.com.  You can follow us on Instagram at https://www.instagram.com/power2tri.multisport/. Last, but not least, we are working on Team Kits, but we need to get to 100 members – if you’re interested join our Zwift Club at https://www.zwift.com/clubs/5819a1e5-7af5-437f-aab7-287c169c0cb2/home.

eventSubgroups.name: Endurance Odyssey - Power2Tri Endurance Coaching (E)

name: Endure IQ Training Squad Session
description:
SESSION FOCUS
The focus of this, indoor, endurance+ ride, is to complete an extended
duration of steady-state focussed blocks of riding in Z2 plus short sprint
intervals targeted at stimulating glycogen breakdown, activation of aerobic
pathways and fat metabolism in the subsequent Zone 2 reps. This approach can
provide two synergistic stimuli that can be particularly effective for the
time crunched athlete unable to fit in separate rides.

These workouts should be done in ERG mode but let HR be your guide for zone 2
intervals, keeping below your zone 2 HR limit where appropriate. For the POWER
sets let ERG control your power in this targeted zone.

From a physiological perspective, these sessions help build power and strength
but also promote fat oxidation, and structural changes at a muscular level,
such as increased mitochondrial content and capillarizatio﻿n while providing a
strong O2 competent as well.

WARM-UP:
Progressive for 15 min
5min at 70%
1min at 50%
15min Z2
3min at 80%
1min at 50%
15min Z2
MAIN SET 1:
12sec reps at L6 (78sec recovery)
MAIN SET 2:
Reps at L2 (5 min recovery)
finish with
5min at 70%
1min at 50%
COOLDOWN:
5 min

name: Team CRYO-GEN Saturday Social!
description:
Join Team CRYO-GEN for a laid-back, social spin every Saturday! We roll for 90 minutes at a friendly D-pace (1.8–2.5 w/kg), perfect for steady endurance, recovery, or just soaking up the good vibes. Whether you’re training, chatting, or just enjoying the ride, you’re in great company here.

Each week, we’ll mix things up with a rotating course, keeping the scenery fresh and the legs guessing. No power-ups needed and no pressure—just a great group of riders and a solid session to kick off your weekend.

Keep it smooth, stay together, and ride on with Team CRYO-GEN!

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: BMTR Flat 100 (Miles)
description:
**Join Us for a Fun and Supportive Group Ride!**

We invite you to join a fun, supportive, and epic group ride. Feel free to join us for as little or as much of the ride as you like; everyone is welcome!

Our experienced, friendly, and engaging Ride Leaders will guide you throughout the ride, ensuring a cohesive group and plenty of entertaining banter.

Please note that while there is no "A" Category Group, we encourage "A Cats" to join the B Category Group, which gives you the option to ride with the B Group "Freebirds," who maintain a faster, more spirited pace than the Beacon Group. (BMTR does not use the “Fence".)

The C and D Groups will make one brief stop at about 62 miles (100 km), while the B Group will not stop along the way. All three categories can use the "Coffee Break" option, allowing a 3-minute break every 30 minutes of riding while you are auto-paced with the group.

This event is designed as a longer endurance ride, lasting 4-5 hours. The average watts per kilogram (w/kg) pace for each group is calculated to keep most riders within their "Zone 2 Endurance / low Zone 3 Tempo" ranges, assuming each rider selects the correct category based on their Functional Threshold Power (FTP) or on Zwift's calculations/assignments.

Since we will primarily use flatter routes* for this event, smaller riders may find themselves at the higher end of their category's average range, while larger riders may fall toward the lower end.

*For our purposes, "flatter routes" are defined as having less than 3,100 feet (945 meters) of climbing per 100 miles (161 km).

***Join Us On Any or All of the Following Platforms!**
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bmtrzwiftclub
- Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/BMTRZwiftClub
- Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/BMTR/home
- Instagram: https://instagram.com/bmtrclub
 
**To Find All Weekly BMTR Events** 
- https://bmtr.events

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Phoenix Rising Soaring Saturday
description:
Please join us on Soaring Saturday and leave all your worries at the door to your pain cave/riding room. We welcome riders who like to go long on the weekend.

The yellow beacon will endeavor to ride at a pace of between 1.5 and 2.0 w/kg. The activation of the no-kick fence will be at the yellow beacon's discretion and used only as a guide for newer riders to let them know they're leaving the main group behind – the objective of the ride, as with all our group rides, is to share a little longer time together and enjoy the camaraderie. We employ a fab group of sweepers to accompany and/or help riders rejoin the main yellow group. We intend that no one ride alone unless they want to do so.

We will ride a variety of routes, hoping to tackle some of the longer and more challenging routes in Zwift so the ride will generally be 120 minutes in length but occasionally be set for distance to allow for a specific route badge. We will not shy away from climbs or non paved surfaces. If you don't have time to complete the longer route(s), you may always leave the ride without quitting it and come back later to complete the route.

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/mNMva8ekJR
Check us out on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/615972979730125/

name: (Rubber-Banded) Ride with James' Friends!
description:
Join (some of) James' friends as we take on a number of different, varied routes across a number of different worlds.

This ride is banded. All riders will stay together regardless of their pace.

To keep up with latest events and news, please join the club here: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/389fbdf0-9cd7-4b42-8f76-4d1b6af240ae

name: In the Zone 2 with DZR
description:
🚴‍♂️Join the Movement and Get In The Zone 2 with DZR🚴‍♂️

It's time to work smarter, not harder, to build your endurance. Zone 2 training targets the sweet spot of aerobic exercise intensity, where you enhance your capacity to burn fat, clear lactate, and ignite new mitochondria – the powerhouses of your cells fueling energy production.

Bid farewell to pushing too hard in a group ride or risking total burnout in a race. The structured workouts are tailored for zone 2 training, eliminating guesswork and overexertion. Open to all levels, our rubberbanded sessions ensure everyone stays together, delivering smart, effective results.

In the Zone 2 with DZR: Sustain without the strain.

More from DZR on our website: www.dzrracingseries.com

name: CTRL Catch Up Climbers
description:
Let's see who can catch up more climbers on Alpe du Zwift route in one hour's time after 5-6km warm-up. Let's run and have some fun.

CTRL is China Triathlon Racing League. Pls find us on TriMore.cn

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: T-VGN 🌱 Route Badge Run
description: If you're missing some run route badges, this is your chance to jump on your treadmill with Team Vegan for our weekly Badge Hunt. These AYOP events will give you the opportunity to complete your collection.

name: Ride to End ALZ
description:
The Ride to End ALZ South Carolina is a three-day, cross-state journey for cycling enthusiasts. Now in its 17th year, the unique Group Ride travels from beautiful Upstate South Carolina to the picturesque Charleston coast July 11-13, 2025. Riders may choose between two mileage options: The Grand Tour (257 miles) or The Half Tour (136 miles). This event is known for its incredible support along the route and camaraderie that only a multi-day ride can bring. Participants may also ride at their own pace from anywhere in our month-long Virtual Challenge. Either way, funds raised by riders benefit the Alzheimer's Association and fuel cutting-edge research targeted to change the trajectory of Alzheimer’s disease. Get all the details at alz.org/ridesc
This ride is open to everyone and all paces- the lead beacon will maintain a steady average D to C pace, and our red beacon will lead a second pace group if necessary! Our goal is to fill you in on the details of the incredible impact Ride to End ALZ has on making progress towards the research and treatment of Alzheimers through funding with the Alzheimer’s Association. In addition to the 3-day Group Ride in SC, there is also a month-long Virtual Challenge option! Join us, unlock the Ride to End ALZ kit, and learn more about how YOU can make a difference in the fight against Alzheimer’s! Join our Facebook group to get all the latest info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/alzRTR

More details at alz.org/ridesc

name: USMeS – Task Force 100 [E]
description:
Task Force 100 - Your Weekly Long Distance Mission

100 Miles on the first Saturday of each month, 100km on all other Saturdays
Every Saturday, we go long. If you’re ready to build serious endurance, this weekly rotation keeps the challenge high and the mileage real. If you’re looking for flat terrain….sorry, but our rides didn’t get that memo 😉

100 Miles (First Saturday each month)
The big one…the Imperial Century.
Time to settle in, fuel well, and grind out 100 miles / 162 km in one steady effort.
Beacons: Two yellow beacons guiding the groups
Fence: No fence—ride your pace, push your limits


100km Ride (All other Saturdays)
The weekly grinder.
Perfect for endurance building 
Ride with the group for 100 km / 62 miles at a smooth, social, consistent pace.
Beacons: Two yellow beacons guiding the groups
Fence: No fence—ride your pace, push your limits

Ride Format
    • Pure endurance—steady, smooth, social miles
    • No pressure—ride the full 100 km / 100 miles or stay as long as you want
    • No fence. Flyers are welcome to fly and everyone is encouraged to choose a sustainable group
    • No scheduled breaks, but coffee refuels every 30 minutes

Expectations
    • Respect all riders—everyone’s here for distance
    • Pace smart—whether 100 km or 100 miles, it’s endurance, not a sprint
    • Support and encourage others as the miles stack up
This is more than a ride. It’s a weekly test of stamina, patience, and love for the long haul.

Because #weareneverflat

Join our community:
Discord: https://discord.gg/ppBc5WKu3m

Search “US Military eSports” on Zwift Clubs, FB, Strava and IG

#conqueringlimits #challengeaccepted

name: HerD Beginner Races
description:
Where do you go to try racing or to grow a little better with your racing craft… Herd Beginner Races is set up to provide you with that opportunity by using Zwift Racing Scores (ZRS) with bandwidths that are set up so you’re competing with people of similarities and routes that enable you to challenge yourself and grow with racing. Our pens are set up as;

Pen A   ZRS  345-430

Pen B   ZRS 265-345

Pen C   ZRS 185-265

Pen D   ZRS 115-185

Pen E   ZRS 0-115

If you’re new to racing please take Zwift’s recommendation in blue.

Each week HBR races will have a different focus; racing on a flat route, rolling/undulating with a small climbs, and a route that is commonly used in many Zwift racing events. In addition, the last race of the month will be Tick-Tock so that you can monitor your performance.

name: Alpe 70 & 85 min Pace & Chase 50/45/40 😈🍍
description:
As Zwift is a great community Torturefest Cult & Team GIANT are collaborating to help you get stronger.

For the Torturefest rides we have added a Twist, stronger riders will be chasing you down. Start times are set so they can catch you in the final segments.

Pen E is the advertised paced ride with beacons
Pen D is a 85 min pace (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen C is the 50 minute pace chase (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen B is the 45 minute pace chase (beacons not guaranteed)
Pen A is the sub 40 minute pace (beacons not guaranteed)

Alpe Paced Rides

Wanna build your Alpe pace but want a wheel to follow?
Join our rides where our beacons will pace you at 4 different advertised
paces across 4 rolling weekends, 2 paces per weekend at 8am CT and 9.30 am CT.

Hold pace until ya crack, then come back again and get to extend the cracking point.

We deliver Personal Records / Bests.

The goal is to build up to the magic sub 60 mins for Alpe, faster and slower riders are always welcome.

This is a rolling 4-week schedule

Weekend #1
8.00 am CT 60 min pace & Chase - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 75 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #2
8.00 am CT 65 min pace & Chase - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 70 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #3
8.00 am CT 70 min pace & Chase - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 65 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #4
8.00 am CT 75 min pace & Chase - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 60 min pace - Team GIANT

Pro tips

Look at your best Alpe time on Strava, there will be a segment for the entire climb with average watts for the entire climb. Beating that average watts number by 5 watts each segment is your goal for each segment.

Use all the tools in the toolbox:
• Cardiovascular = Seated higher rpm
• Legs = Seated low rpm – Great for the knees!!!
• Legs = Standing low rpm – You will crack

We go warm for the first 25%, a steady pace for the until 75% and push at the end. Start out too hot and you’ll suffer at the top.

name: ZSUN Base Builder 2
description:
Join one of ZSUN’s longest-running and most loved events – BaseBuilder 2. Designed to help you build or maintain aerobic endurance, this ride typically covers 100km (shorter for E pen) at a consistent Zone 2 pace, with friendly chat and helpful advice throughout.

Expect a welcoming atmosphere, steady pacing (apart from the ups and downs), and a large group of regular riders. Drafting is essential, so stay close and work together!

🗓️
Saturday | ~1.8–3.2 w/kg | 90–120 mins

💬
Regular chat & tips | Join Discord for voice comms

🎁
Complete the ride to earn your ZSUN kit

🏁
Pens A–E available | All riders welcome

⚠️
Fence may be used to keep the group together

Whether you're building fitness or enjoying the social side, this ride has it all.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (A)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 3.3 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.7 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 3.0 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (B)
eventSubgroups.description: Lead by me Scott McVeigh aka Nanny! - The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.7 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.5 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 2.5 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (C)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.3 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 3.0 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 2.0 w/kg. We try to stay together allowing us to move faster as a large pack.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (D)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 2.0 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 2.5 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 1.7 w/kg.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN Base Builder 2 (E)
eventSubgroups.description: The ride targets people who are looking to maintain an average pace of 1.6 w/kg. To 1.8 w/kg. On short climbs we will usually stand and push to about 2.3 w/kg and on the downhills we relax and recover a bit at 1.5 w/kg. The Sweeper in this Pen will hold 1.4 w/kg. This Pen may be a shorter distance than the rest each week.

name: Giant Bagel Rolls Ride
description:
Join the Giant Bagel Rolls ride. A fun high-C endurance ride. Steady pace and banter included 

The ride will be a steady 2.6-2.8 endurance ride for 100 km. Social cruise, with plenty of chat and banter.
Bring along good humor, respect for others, and a willingness to work for the team to help individuals achieve their endurance goals – whether that be a first-time century, a recovery ride, or a chillax.

name: 111 CIEONTONCE v-race
description: Bienvenidos al 111 - Strongman racing series, una carrera virtual que tiene como fin mantener alta la motivación, entrenar de forma entretenida, y compartir con personas de Colombia y otros países. Tendremos rifas, premios y sorpresas para los participantes que completen el evento.

name: Catrina's Endurance Ride
description:
I would like to invite ALL women to join this Ride & Social exchange! Our goals during the ride are as follows:

Socialize with other women as well as complete a goal of riding a 100 miles on Zwift or just doing a long endurance training ride together with other women.  

This is NOT a NO DROP ride.  With that said we do try in my rides to keep the pace steady and drop back if we know early enough to bring back dropped riders.  We encourage all women to join to get stronger together. 

Ultimately my goal is to enhance your endurance training while having FUN!

Catrina Weiss (Group Leader)

We look forward to meeting & riding with you ladies as always!

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Drafting Dinos Race Team)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Drafting Dinos Race Team (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b8db3780-08e2-4ac7-87dd-d9a4f5fc4240).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Drafting Dinos Race Team) (E)

eventSeries.name: ZRL 25/26 R4
eventSeries.description: Zwift Racing League Season 20 (2025/26 - Round 4). Includes all races and official recon rides

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Slowpoke Soiree p/b Ascenders
description:
Our Team would like to cordially invite you to join our nice weekend club ride that will cruise at a slower speed of 15mph/24kph range. This is designed to be an enjoyable event for you to ride with like minded people.

While this will be an all out effort for some, we have kind and helpful riders who are there to lend a wheel whenever needed. For others, it may be just right or a recovery pace. Whatever your reason may be for joining us, we look forward to you give us a try.

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: Bike MS Community Challenge
description:
Bike MS Season if right around the corner, so let's connect over a ride as we prepare for the adventures ahead. Multiple Sclerosis does not stop, so neither will we as we ride together for this great cause.
Our monthly events are mirrored after the Bike MS Rides hosted by the National MS Society in which each day riders can choose their own adventure and challenge. Whatever distance ride you choose, come join a group of passionate cyclists looking to raise funds and awareness for those living with multiple sclerosis. All Zwift riders are welcome as we ride for a world free of MS. We hope you enjoy the ride and if so, please join Bike MS Zwift Club for year round training and community engagement. Come for the miles of smiles and stay for the XP. 
Bike MS is the largest charity cycling series across the United States consisting of 50 rides and raising over $50 million in 2023 for the National MS Society. Bike MS supports the National MS Society's mission to Cure MS while empowering all people affected by MS to live their best lives. Whether you want to cross the Finish Line in person or take on a new challenge from anywhere — there’s a Bike MS event that’s right for you! 
Check out www.bikeMS.org and experience the ride of your life. Use Zwift24 for a discount on your Bike MS Ride entry fee.
Come join us for weekly Club Rides: Bike MS ZC
#dontjustride #bikeMS #whateverittakes #strongertogether

name: Run In The Park
description:
Come and join the original Zwift group run! 

This event is a 5km mass start event and runs at multiple times every Saturday.  

It is open to runners of all abilities so whether you are a recreational runner or an elite athlete you are welcome on the start line.  

Get to the start gates early to chat with the other runners, you may find someone of a similar ability to group up with for the run.  

There is no time limit for completing the event so run at your own pace and enjoy the course.

name: Sedate Runners Group
description:
Do you run slowly or are you a runner who is just starting out?  Are you a runner who wants to do relaxed runs, enjoying the moments in the company of others?  Do you want to run without any pressure and without worrying about being left behind?  If so, this is definitely the group for you.  Please join us. 

We will have great fun together.  There is no set pace, and we can promise you that you will be able to run comfortably, and in the company of others like you.  No runner will be left behind. 

led by Sophia Skyers and Laura Colclough

Facebook Group:	https//www.facebook.com/group.SedateRunnersGroup

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: ZLDR Weekly Long Run
description:
Welcome to the ZLDR Weekly Long Run. ZLDR is a group of diverse runners, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners who will make sure that you are never left behind.

This distance of this run changes depending on the week. For most Saturdays, the event distance will be 13.1 miles/21.1 km. On the first Saturday of every month our run will go to 26.2 miles / 42.2 km -- that’s a full marathon. We keep this run on routes that are faily short loops so that everyone can stay within chat distance of each other no matter what speed you are running or walking.

Of course, as with all of our runs, you are not required to go the entire distance listed. Feel free to run as long as you would like, but as a distance running group we like to offer our members a chance every month to put their legs to the test and see how far they can go.

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Strava:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: Triathletes on Zwift Saturday Endurance Workout
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This workout is part of a 30-week progressive series that builds from tempo to sub threshold focused workout efforts.  The aim of this series is to build riders long ride aerobic abilities, specifically to enable them to effectively get off the bike prepared to run.  Total ride time over the 24 build series weeks will extend from 1hr :15 min up to 3 hours.  As workouts get longer we will incorporate higher effort/shorter duration intervals (super threshold work) into the program after the main set.  These workouts are perfect for long course focused triathletes along with anyone interested in developing their abilities to ride for longer periods of time.  Each of the 24-week workouts builds off the preceding workout in terms of duration of main set intervals and total ride time.

WORKOUT DESIGNER

AJ Baucco is a well established professional triathlete from Cleveland, Ohio. He founded AJ Baucco Coaching, which has coached over 150 triathletes in the last 5 years. He also manages 3 different age group triathlon teams; The Baucco Squad, TRS Racing, and Team INFINIT Performance. Over the years, AJ Baucco has become a premier triathlon coach. He has helped athletes achieve personal bests, stand on podiums, and even race world championship events. On the race course, AJ Baucco has achieved top results at every distance from super sprint to Ironman. Most recently, he was on the winning team for 6 different Major League Triathlon events, and 2 back to back series championships.

Contact – 
Websites – www.baucco.com, www.ajbcoaching.com, www.bauccosquad.com
Twitter - @irunshirtless
Instagram - @ajbaucco

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins.  Everyone stays together regardless of power output.   That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other.  Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level.   

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

eventSubgroups.name: Triathletes on Zwift Saturday Endurance Workout (E)

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: DIRT Dadurday Chase Race
description:
Welcome to the DIRT Dadurday Chase Race.

Race category starts will be staggered with the E category starting first, and each D, C, B, and A category starting later.

The riders will be challenged to work together using the draft, and group tactics in order to catch the category ahead of them while also holding off the categories that started after. 

Races will have 4 power-ups enabled with equal odds (25% each): 
draft, feather, burrito, & ghost! 
Compete with the best, and see how you stack up!

You must be opted-in on Zwift.com & ZwiftPower.com to be counted for race results.
We highly encourage A racers to follow Zwift eRacing Ruleset.
Dual recording of trainer & power meter, and recent weigh-in on ZwifTransparency 
recommended--we reserve the right to ask for this information to verify outstanding 
performances.

Categories are based on Zwift Racing Score:
A: 800 - 1000
B: 600 - 800
C: 400 - 600
D: 200 - 400
E: 0 - 200

name: Team CLS Base 'n' Banter
description:
Base ’n’ Banter is a controlled weekend endurance ride - with a strong community and social focus! 

Across every weekend in 2026, we will continue to offer a mix of rides that cover flat, rolling and hilly terrain with the aim of getting in a solid endurance ride, mostly around 2hrs.

This is a chance to enjoy a friendly experienced group club ride with regulars who have no Big Egos. While you build your fitness, there is always plenty of in-ride banter. 

What pace to expect?
We will ride the flats around 2.5 - 2.7 w/kg with a focus on keeping everyone together!

The leader will climb at around 3.0 - 3.2 w/kg depending on the length and if needed we will always try and regroup after.

While the leader will do everything to keep everyone together, we can't slow repeatedly so some splits are inevitable.

Our SWEEPERS on this ride, known as our ‘ORANGE ARMY’ (OA), are members of TEAM CLS who are dedicated to helping others. If you have had a drop out or accidentally hit a wrong key, type ‘HELP’ and they will come back and offer you a wheel back to the group.

Note: While we always try to ensure this ride is led by one of our beacons there may be some weeks where this is not possible. The ride always has regulars that will ride to the advertised pace!


WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social



TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426



TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: 3R Endurance Steady Ride
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.
#3r

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home
