name: Ride On with Team Type 1 Social Ride
description:
The ride duration is 60 minutes and the ride leader will strictly adhere to a steady 1.5-2.0w/kg pace.

ZWIFT Team Type 1 is affiliated with Team Type 1 Foundation and comprised of riders with Type 1 Diabetes or who have a close connection to someone with the condition. If Type 1 Diabetes touches your life please consider joining our ZWIFT team at https://www.facebook.com/groups/1365148016828687/

We are focused on building a community to support one another and to raise awareness within the greater ZWIFT community by riding and/or racing together. We are excited to have you join us!! Ride on.

TAKE ACTION/DONATIONS
Fulfilling the TT1 mission is possible only through your help to save children within developing countries. Even now, in the 21st century, many still suffer and die from diabetes, yet all that is needed are the tools and medication to manage the disease. Your donation also will provide continuing studies for athletes with diabetes, who want to take care of their own health while educating others about how best to do the same.
http://teamtype1.org/take-action-donations/

#teamtype1

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: CCBoucherville-V2000 FUN Races
description:
Club Cycliste Boucherville (CCB) in Quebec (Canada) is a non-profit organization to promote cycling as a social and healthy lifestyle. We help kids to acquire the knowledge and skills that will allow to develop themselves as cyclists. Although we prioritize the pleasure at training, we also make sure to support those who wish to participate in races, regardless of the objectives. Young riders can join us from the age of 5... as long as they want to have fun on two wheels. The first year, we can even provide them with a bike for free - thanks to Vélo-2000 Garneau Concept (https://velo2000.qc.ca/)).

This weekly race is meant to be an opportunity to ride and have fun with teammates and opponents during the cold season, and meet passionate cyclists from around the world.

Race your category (based on your best estimation of your FTP/kg).

- A: 4.0W/kg and OVER (Red Circle Icon)  --  3 laps
- B: 3.2W/kg to 3.99W/kg (Green Circle Icon)  --  3 laps
- C: 2.5W/kg to 3.19W/kg (Blue Circle Icon)  --  2 laps
- D: UNDER 2.5W/kg (Yellow Circle Icon)  --  1 lap

Be prepared for 1, 2 or 3 laps (around 10 km each). The course is relatively flat. Start strong and grab a good wheel.

All bikes allowed

PowerUp allowed

Heart Rate Monitor recommended

Unfair and unsporting behaviour prohibited

Fun and Lactate mandatory

Results will be available here and on zwiftpower.com. These races do not count towards any league standings.

Join us on our Discord Channel (https://discord.gg/DZDVFVhto) to discuss your race strategy during the warm-up… and chat during the race if you have enough energy. Use the GENERAL channel, or discuss more privately with your Bike Club teammates in your respective channel. Welcome to French- and English-speaking riders. Please use push-to-talk. 

Please visit our website https://clubcyclisteboucherville.com/ and our FaceBook page https://www.facebook.com/clubcyclisteboucherville/ to share pictures, comments and suggestions.

Ride Hard. Have Fun.

#sympatique #CCB #velo2000 #FunRace

name: Team DRAFT Saturday Race
description:
Welcome to the Saturday Crit Race brought to you by Team DRAFT!
We encourage that all participants head over to zwiftpower.com and register. Riders who have not completed the Zwiftpower registration will not be included in the official results. We also ask that your Zwift activities be saved publicly, and that you open and maintain a Strava account to allow for organizer analysis.
RULES:
- No zPower riders.
- Heart rate monitors mandatory for all categories
- 5min and 20min CEVAZ limits apply. Riding in the wrong category will lead to disqualification.
RACE CATEGORIES:
A: 4.0 W/kg to 5.0 W/kg
B: 3.2 W/kg to 3.99 W/kg
C: 2.5 W/kg to 3.19 W/kg
D: Under 2.49 W/kg

name: Applewood Cycling Saturday Chase Race
description:
If you enjoy the Applewood Manor Cycling weekly group ride, and you are ready to take advantage of your Douce course riding skills and put them to the race test then join Applewood Manor Cycling for our Saturday epic race.  

Double the distance and double the challenge of our popular group ride.   Take on 4 laps of the Douce course covering approximately 100km in just over 2 hours.    Similar to our weekly group ride, everyone will start together and fly around the course with a little help from #doubledraft.    

This is one of the fastest long distance races on Zwift with average speeds close to 48kph for the A group.   

Please join our Applewood Cycling club on Strava and follow us  Instagram @applewood_manor.  Visit our Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/theapplewoodmanor for information about our B&B and our cycling program and to leave comments and suggestions.  Website: www.applewoodmanor.com

name: EVR Winter Series
description:
Welcome to the EVR Winter Series. During these novel times where
outdoor, in-person racing is not possible, we wanted to provide a continued
sense of community through organizing a weekly race series to allow
everyone the chance to race together, albeit indoors separately. We'll keep
track of your efforts as you keep training and racing indoors - you just
keep coming back for more!

Give us a shout if this is your first time here and don't know what to do.
For the experienced, we suggest the typical Zwift recommendations for power:

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-6.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg
E: Women Only (all w/kg)

eventSubgroups.name: EVR Winter Series (A)
eventSubgroups.description: #doubledraft

name: Alpe 65 min Pace 😈🍍
description:
As Zwift is a great community Torturefest Cult & Team GIANT are collaborating
to help you get stronger.

Wanna build your Alpe pace but want a wheel to follow?
Join our rides where our beacons will pace you at 4 different advertised paces across 4 rolling weekends, 2 paces per weekend at 8am CT and 9.30am CT.

We do have unofficial slower and faster paces just ask in the pen and if we have available pacers we will pace a 2nd pace as well.

Hold pace until ya crack, then come back again and get to extend the cracking point. 
We deliver Personal Records / Bests.

The goal is to build up to the magic sub 60 mins for Alpe, faster and slower riders are always welcome.

This is a rolling 4-week schedule 

Weekend #1
8.00 am CT 60 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 75 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #2
8.00 am CT 65 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 70 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #3
8.00 am CT 70 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 65 min pace - Team GIANT

Weekend #4
8.00 am CT 75 min pace - Torturefest Cult
9.30 am CT 60 min pace - Team GIANT


Pro tips

1) Look at your best Alpe time on Strava, there will be a segment for the
entire climb with average watts for the entire climb. Beating that average watt
number by 5 watts each segment is your goal for each segment.

2) Use all the tools in the toolbox:

• Cardiovascular = Seated higher rpm

• Legs = Seated low rpm – Great for the knees!!!!

• Legs = Standing low rpm – You will crack

3) We go warm for the first 25%, a steady pace for the until 75% and push
at the end, start out to hot you’ll suffer at the top.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

description:
Welcome to GALAXY and our Summits and Switchbacks group ride . This weekly ride will target climbing , undulating and occasionally flat and badge hunt courses too.
Jump on, settle in and respect the leader’s pace and instructions, the time or distance will fly by!

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO race series. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: PACK🐺 SUB2 + KOM After Party
description:
This PACK SUB2 event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a friendly group ride experience with a pace that matches the description of less than 2w/kg. It’s a great introduction to group riding with full pace instructions via text and voice chat using discord. You can sign up early via the web or mobile app and late join is enabled.

After the event finishes, there is an optional fun After Party KOM challenge up the final climb. Help pace others or try to beat your HoloReplay.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

description:

Tiny Races are mean to be raced as set of 4 back-to-back events that finish within one hour. Each race is on a different route that takes ~7-12 minutes to complete, and races are scheduled 15 minutes apart.

3 RULES:
- No skipping then returning. You will receive a DQ if you skip race 1, 2, or 3 but finish a later race in the set.

name: Saris Fresh Legs
description:
Welcome to the Saris Fresh Legs Ride! 

This group ride is an introduction to indoor riding & training for those new to the virtual world. The Saris mission is to break down barriers so that everyone has access to fall in love with the bike. These rides are an unintimidating gateway into the world of virtual riding. 

We'll keep a social pace between 0.5 and 1.5 w/kg and each ride will be led by a Saris employee or ambassador who will keep the rides fun & interactive. If you're new to riding indoors and have questions feel free to use the Zwift Companion App to ask them during the ride.

This Saris kit will be unlocked for everyone who finishes the ride!

name: Bicycle Way of Life Saturday Ride
description:
Bicycle Way of Life Saturday Morning 100K

This group ride is not a race, rather it is a ride intended as a D level opportunity for a metric century.  Our pace can best be described as endurance pace, typically on a relatively flat course.  We always plan to provide lead and sweep support, but there will be times where that will not be the case.  You are encouraged to pace with the ride leader/yellow beacon, but if they are not able to make the ride, or if you find the pace too quick, drop off, group up, work together and find your way to 100K.  Teamwork makes this ride work.
 
Want to know more about us? Bicycle Way of Life (bicycleway.com) is a local bike store chain in Eugene, Oregon. We encourage new and experienced riders to support each other indoors and out, fixing trails or just their bikes.
Interact and find more info. by joining our FB group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bwolzwift
Look us up on Strava https://www.strava.com/clubs/bicycleway-com, Instagram https://www.instagram.com/bicyclewayoflife/, and Facebook https://www.facebook.com/bicycleway/

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: NoPinz R3R - 60km Race
description:
Welcome to this NoPinz R3R Race. The rules are simple....
Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.
Cats A, and B will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.
Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual (power) record and to make analysis publicly available on ZwiftPower; they must also be using a direct drive trainer or power meter.
**Elite level performances are also expected to be supported by dual (power) recordings from reputable power sources or easily verifiable outside data. This applies to those with a history of such performances.
** 5.2 w/kg (20 mins); 6 w/kg (5 mins); 9.8 w/kg (1 min)
Zwift Activities must be saved publicly.
Please also maintain an open and active Strava profile to facilitate organiser inquiry.
Please see http://www.zwiftpower.com for finalised race results. The race is brought to you by NoPinz please visit https://nopinz.com/product-tag/subzero/ All subzero kit can be custom made in any design.

name: T-VGN 🌱 Sprout & Sprint
description: Welcome to Team Vegan’s Sunday Sprout & Sprint race. This is a chance to test yourself with race events each week. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

name: Fearless Womens 5K Race
description:
What's your best 5K time? Challenge yourself against other Zwift women runners and find out what you are made of!

One run can change your day, many runs can change your life.

Join to challenge yourself and for the race fun!!!

Hosted by Team Fearless. To hear about our other rides and runs or learn more about our women's team, head to our Facebook Fans page at https://www.facebook.com/groups/TeamFearlessZR/

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: Velos Race Of Truth
description:
This energy-sapping event pits riders against each other in a race they cycle alone. The individual time trial is referred to as the 'race of truth' because it reveals your own strength without the aid of draft from fellow riders. With breakneck speed valued above everything else, prepare to be amazed by this fast-paced spectacle.

Race Rules and Information
Race the same course each month to easily establish your improvement each week.
Riders will enter the category allowed by Zwift category Enforcement.
TT Bikes only
Only Riders in your category will be visible and without any draft. Z power and riders without heart rate monitors will be removed from the results.
Completing the race will unlock the Team Velos kit.

Join our community at www.teamvelos.co.uk

name: Tiny Race (4 of 4) by Zwift Insider
description:
IMPORTANT: read these notes to perform your best and avoid disqualification!

Tiny Races are mean to be raced as set of 4 back-to-back events that finish within one hour. Each race is on a different route that takes ~7-12 minutes to complete, and races are scheduled 15 minutes apart.

3 RULES:
- No skipping then returning. You will receive a DQ if you skip race 1, 2, or 3 but finish a later race in the set.
- You must have an active ZwiftPower account to compete for the overall podium.
- Smart trainer or power meter is required.

THIS WEEK'S ROUTES:
Race 1: Spinfinity (6.043km, ends atop escalator leaving City Hall Station) - Powerup: Feather
Race 2: Watts the Limit (6.49km, ends at bottom of City Hall Station entrance ramp) - Powerup: Anvil
Race 3: Double Span Spin (5.565km, ends at Manhattan Sprint arch) - Powerup: Draft Boost
Race 4: Fuhgeddaboudit (7.162km, ends leaving subway tube) - Powerup: Aero Boost

PRO TIPS: 
You will receive a single powerup at the start of each race, as listed above. Be sure to visit https://zwiftinsider.com/tiny/ for details on this week's routes, including custom finish line locations.

RESULTS:
You earn points in each race based on your finishing position. The overall category winners each week (available on ZwiftPower) are the riders with the most points across all 4 races. ZwiftPower result links:
- Zone 1 (10am UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1172
- Zone 2 (4pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1173
- Zone 3 (10pm UTC): https://zwiftpower.com/league.php?id=1174

name: Saturday Masters Trilogy (1/3) - Team INOX
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍💨 INOX Masters Trilogy  by Team INOX ⚡️🚴🏼‍💨
• You must have an active Zwift Power account on https://zwiftpower.com
• Heart rate monitors are mandatory for podium positions
• Smart trainers or power meters must be connected as the power source to participate in races
• Enter the correct age category
Make sure you meet all requirements to avoid disqualification

INOX Masters Trilogy will have a double weekly appointment: Saturday and Sunday; in which three races will be run every 20 minutes
To ensure balanced and fair competition the starts will be simultaneous with the cyclists visible to each other categories and the 5 ages start categories will be:
Elite (Cat A) → Up to 29 years old
M30 (Cat B) → 30 to 39 years old
M40 (Cat C) → 40 to 49 years old
M50 (Cat D) → 50 to 59 years old
M60+ (Cat E) → 60 years old and up

Race Type: SCRATCH
Bike Upgrade Enabled
Steering Enabled
Power-Ups: NONE
Jersey Unlock: FREESPEED

Every day of race will have a ranking time of all three races
The ranking will be divided into 10 categories based on the age groups:
A = Under 35
B = 35 - 39
C = 40 - 44
D = 45 - 49
E = 50 - 54
F = 55- 59
G = 60 -64
H = 65 - 69
I = 70 - 74
J = Over 75

More info will be communicated promptly via the Discord and Facebook channels
The organizers reserve the right to carry out checks in case of suspected misconduct and if necessary cancel the performances obtained

Everyone is Welcome! 🙏🏻
